questions.question: Which of the following is a vector quantity?
questions.explanation: Displacement has both magnitude and direction, making it a vector. Speed, distance, and mass are scalars.

questions.question: A car travels 40 km East, then 30 km North. What is the magnitude of its displacement?
Which of these quantities is a scalar?
questions.explanation: Work has only magnitude (measured in joules), no direction. The others are vectors.

questions.question: If vector a + vector b = 0, then:
options: |a| = |b| and they are in the same direction
|a| = |b| and they are in opposite directions
questions.explanation: For two vectors to sum to zero, they must have equal magnitudes but opposite directions.

questions.question: The dot product of two perpendicular vectors is:
Which is NOT a vector quantity?
questions.explanation: Energy (whether kinetic or potential) is a scalar quantity with no direction.

questions.question: A man walks 3 m North, then 4 m East, then 3 m South. His displacement is:
questions.explanation: North and South cancel (3-3=0). Net displacement is 4 m East.

questions.question: The magnitude of vector A = 3î + 4ĵ is:
Distance is to displacement as speed is to:
questions.explanation: Distance and speed are scalars; displacement and velocity are their vector counterparts.

questions.question: Two vectors of magnitude 5 N and 12 N act at right angles. The magnitude of their resultant is:
questions.explanation: For perpendicular vectors: R = √(5² + 12²) = √(25+144) = √169 = 13 N

questions.question: A car accelerates from rest at 4 m/s² for 10 seconds. What is its final velocity?
An object is dropped from rest and falls for 3 seconds. How far does it fall? (g = 10 m/s²)
A car moving at 20 m/s accelerates at 2 m/s² for 5 seconds. Its final velocity is:
An object accelerates from 10 m/s to 30 m/s over a distance of 100 m. The acceleration is:
A stone is thrown vertically upward with initial velocity 20 m/s. What is its maximum height? (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: At max height, v = 0. Using v² = u² - 2gh → 0 = 20² - 2(10)h → h = 400/20 = 20 m

questions.question: The area under a velocity-time graph represents:
questions.explanation: Area under v-t graph = velocity × time = displacement

questions.question: A car travels 180 km in 3 hours. Its average speed is:
questions.explanation: Average speed = total distance / total time = 180/3 = 60 km/h

questions.question: Which equation represents uniformly accelerated motion?
questions.explanation: This is the first equation of motion for constant acceleration.

questions.question: An object starts from rest and travels 72 m in 6 s. Its acceleration is:
How long does it take an object to fall 80 m from rest? (g = 10 m/s²)
A car decelerates uniformly from 25 m/s to rest in 5 seconds. The deceleration is:
questions.explanation: a = (v-u)/t = (0-25)/5 = -5 m/s². Magnitude of deceleration = 5 m/s²

questions.question: The slope of a distance-time graph gives:
questions.explanation: Slope of s-t graph = Δs/Δt = speed (or velocity if direction considered)

questions.question: An object thrown upward with velocity 30 m/s will reach maximum height in: (g = 10 m/s²)
For an object moving with constant velocity:
questions.explanation: Constant velocity means no change in velocity, hence acceleration = 0

questions.question: A ball is thrown horizontally from a cliff at 15 m/s. After 2 s, its horizontal displacement is:
questions.explanation: Horizontal: x = vt = 15(2) = 30 m (no horizontal acceleration)

questions.question: A 5 kg object accelerates at 8 m/s². The net force acting on it is:
The weight of a 10 kg mass on Earth where g = 10 m/s² is:
When the net force on an object is zero, the object:
options: Must be moving with constant velocity
Is either at rest or moving with constant velocity
questions.explanation: Newton's first law: zero net force means zero acceleration, so v is constant (which includes v = 0)

questions.question: A 2 kg object is subjected to a net force of 14 N. Its acceleration is:
A 10 kg block slides down a 30° incline. The component of weight parallel to the plane is: (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: F_parallel = mgsinθ = 10 × 10 × sin(30°) = 100 × 0.5 = 50 N

questions.question: Action and reaction forces:
questions.explanation: Newton's third law: action-reaction pairs act on different objects

questions.question: Friction always acts:
options: Opposite to the direction of motion
questions.explanation: Friction opposes relative motion or attempted motion

questions.question: A car of mass 1000 kg accelerates from rest to 20 m/s in 10 s. The average force is:
The force of friction depends on:
options: Normal force and coefficient of friction
questions.explanation: f = μN where μ is coefficient of friction and N is normal force

questions.question: If the mass of an object is doubled while the net force remains constant, its acceleration:
questions.explanation: a = F/m. If m doubles, a becomes half

questions.question: A 50 N horizontal force is applied to a 10 kg block on a frictionless surface. The acceleration is:
The normal force on an object resting on a horizontal surface equals:
questions.explanation: On horizontal surface with no vertical acceleration, N = mg = W

questions.question: An object of mass 4 kg rests on a surface with μ = 0.2. The maximum static friction is: (g = 10 m/s²)
questions.explanation: f_max = μN = μmg = 0.2 × 4 × 10 = 8 N

questions.question: Two masses m₁ = 4 kg and m₂ = 6 kg hang on a frictionless pulley. The acceleration of the system is: (g = 10 m/s²)
A 10 kg object is pushed with 100 N force. If friction is 40 N, the acceleration is:
The work done in lifting a 5 kg mass through 10 m is: (g = 10 m/s²)
A 2 kg object moving at 6 m/s has kinetic energy:
The potential energy of a 10 kg mass at height 5 m is: (g = 10 m/s²)
An object falls from height h. Just before hitting the ground, its kinetic energy equals:
questions.explanation: All PE converts to KE: KE = PE_initial = mgh

questions.question: Work done is measured in:
questions.explanation: SI unit of work (and energy) is the joule (J)

questions.question: Power is defined as:
questions.explanation: P = W/t = rate of doing work

questions.question: A 20 N force moves an object 4 m in the direction of the force. Work done is:
An engine does 5000 J of work in 20 s. Its power output is:
If the speed of an object doubles, its kinetic energy:
questions.explanation: KE ∝ v². If v doubles, KE increases by factor of 2² = 4

questions.question: Work done by friction is:
questions.explanation: Friction opposes motion, so work done by friction (on moving object) is negative

questions.question: A compressed spring stores:
questions.explanation: Compressed or stretched springs store elastic PE

questions.question: The work-energy theorem states:
questions.explanation: Net work done equals change in kinetic energy

questions.question: A 5 kg object moving at 4 m/s increases speed to 8 m/s. The work done is:
At the highest point of a pendulum's swing:
options: KE is maximum, PE is minimum
KE is minimum, PE is maximum
Both KE and PE are maximum
Both KE and PE are zero
questions.explanation: At highest point, velocity is zero (KE = 0 = minimum) and height is maximum (PE is maximum)

questions.question: The SI unit of power is:
questions.explanation: Power is measured in watts (W), where 1 W = 1 J/s

questions.question: An object of mass m is raised to height h, then released. Its speed just before hitting ground is:
questions.explanation: mgh = (1/2)mv² → v = √(2gh)

questions.question: Work done is zero when:
questions.explanation: W = Fscosθ. When θ = 90°, cos90° = 0, so W = 0

questions.question: A machine lifts 100 kg through 20 m in 10 s. The power is: (g = 10 m/s²)
Total mechanical energy is conserved when:
questions.explanation: Mechanical energy is conserved only when all forces are conservative (no friction/air resistance)

questions.question: If KE of an object is 200 J and PE is 300 J, total mechanical energy is:
questions.explanation: Total ME = KE + PE = 200 + 300 = 500 J

questions.question: A 4 kg object moving at 5 m/s has momentum:
questions.explanation: p = mv = 4 × 5 = 20 kg·m/s

questions.question: In an isolated system, total momentum is:
questions.explanation: Law of conservation of momentum applies when no external forces act

questions.question: Two objects of mass 3 kg and 5 kg move with velocities 4 m/s and 2 m/s respectively in the same direction. After collision, they stick together. Their common velocity is:
Impulse equals:
questions.explanation: I = Ft = Δp (change in momentum)

questions.question: A 2 kg ball moving at 10 m/s strikes a wall and bounces back at 8 m/s. The change in momentum is:
questions.explanation: Taking initial direction as positive: Δp = m(v_f - v_i) = 2(-8 - 10) = 2(-18) = -36 kg·m/s. Magnitude = 36 kg·m/s

questions.question: In a perfectly inelastic collision:
questions.explanation: Perfectly inelastic collision means objects stick together and move with common velocity

questions.question: A 50 N force acts on a body for 0.1 s. The impulse is:
Momentum is conserved in:
questions.explanation: Momentum is always conserved in isolated systems, regardless of collision type

questions.question: Two trolleys of mass 3 kg and 2 kg moving at 4 m/s and 3 m/s in opposite directions collide and stick. Their common velocity is:
questions.explanation: Taking first direction as positive: 3(4) + 2(-3) = 5v → 12 - 6 = 5v → v = 1.2 m/s

questions.question: In an elastic collision:
options: Both momentum and KE are conserved
questions.explanation: Elastic collisions conserve both momentum and kinetic energy

questions.question: The SI unit of momentum is:
questions.explanation: Momentum = mass × velocity, so units are kg·m/s (also equivalent to N·s)

questions.question: A rocket works on the principle of:
questions.explanation: Rocket propulsion is based on conservation of momentum (action-reaction)

questions.question: A 1000 kg car moving at 20 m/s collides with a stationary 2000 kg truck. If they stick together, their velocity is:
If the velocity of an object is doubled, its momentum:
questions.explanation: p = mv is linear in v, so doubling v doubles p

questions.question: A force of 100 N acts for 0.5 s on a 5 kg mass initially at rest. The final velocity is:
The centripetal acceleration of an object in circular motion is given by:
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration formula: a_c = v²/r

questions.question: A mass of 2 kg revolves in a circle of radius 4 m at 6 m/s. The centripetal force is:
The period of circular motion is related to speed by:
questions.explanation: Period T = circumference/speed = 2πr/v

questions.question: Centripetal force always acts:
questions.explanation: Centripetal means 'center-seeking'; force points toward center

questions.question: An object moves in a circle at constant speed. Its acceleration is:
options: Constant in magnitude, changing in direction
Constant in direction, changing in magnitude
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration has constant magnitude but continuously changes direction

questions.question: Angular velocity ω and linear velocity v are related by:
questions.explanation: Linear velocity v = ωr where ω is in rad/s

questions.question: A satellite orbits Earth due to:
questions.explanation: Gravity provides the necessary centripetal force for orbital motion

questions.question: If the radius of circular path is halved while speed remains constant, centripetal acceleration:
questions.explanation: a_c = v²/r. If r is halved, a_c doubles

questions.question: The direction of centripetal acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration points toward the center of circular path

questions.question: For a conical pendulum, the centripetal force is provided by:
questions.explanation: The horizontal component of string tension provides centripetal force

questions.question: Which coordinate system uses (r, θ, φ)?
questions.explanation: Spherical coordinates use radius r, polar angle θ, and azimuthal angle φ

questions.question: The dimension of force is:
questions.explanation: [F] = [ma] = M × LT⁻² = MLT⁻²

questions.question: Convert 72 km/h to m/s:
Which is a fundamental quantity?
questions.explanation: Time is a fundamental quantity; others are derived

questions.question: The dot product of two vectors gives a:
questions.explanation: Dot product (scalar product) yields a scalar quantity

questions.question: Which law states that for every action there is an equal and opposite reaction?
questions.explanation: This is Newton's third law

questions.question: Hooke's law for springs states:
questions.explanation: Force in spring is proportional to extension: F = kx

questions.question: The efficiency of a machine is:
questions.explanation: Efficiency = (Useful output/Total input) × 100%

questions.question: Relative velocity of A with respect to B is:
questions.explanation: v_AB = v_A - v_B

questions.question: The slope of a velocity-time graph gives:
questions.explanation: Slope of v-t graph = Δv/Δt = acceleration

questions.question: Density is defined as:
questions.explanation: ρ = m/V

questions.question: The cross product of two parallel vectors is:
questions.explanation: A × B = |A||B|sinθ. For parallel vectors, θ = 0°, so sin0° = 0

questions.question: Simple harmonic motion occurs when acceleration is proportional to:
questions.explanation: In SHM, a ∝ -x (proportional and opposite to displacement)

questions.question: The range of a projectile is maximum when launched at:
questions.explanation: Maximum range occurs at 45° for same initial and final heights

questions.question: Inertia depends on:
questions.explanation: Inertia is the resistance to change in motion, which depends only on mass
